source: Summarized from the escbase template workflow, starter modules, and visual-pattern notes.

globalRules.safeZone: For 9:16, keep important content inside padding top 100px, left/right 28px, bottom 200px.
globalRules.visualFirst: Voiceover/subtitles carry explanation. Canvas should use short labels, icons, metrics, source media, diagrams, and motion.
globalRules.revealMapping: Each narration sentence or SRT beat should map to one semantic reveal: one metric, card, icon, media focus, or diagram step.
globalRules.textDensity: Avoid paragraphs. Prefer 1-5 word labels, numbers, logos, and symbols. Do not copy the full narration.
globalRules.composition: Fill the safe area with one strong visual idea. Avoid sparse scenes and repeated card stacks.
globalRules.assets: Use only local media catalog src values. Prefer brand/logo assets when a brand or product is named.
globalRules.sourceDiscipline: Separate verified source facts from commentary or opinion. When a number comes from a company post, README, or article, phrase it as source-reported unless independently verified.

script: Hook must make the topic clear in the first beat.
Each scene should carry one central idea; split the scene if it needs multiple explanations.
Each sentence should carry one revealable idea and read naturally in the selected video language.
Do not overclaim source facts; distinguish source-reported facts from analysis, market interpretation, or community reaction.
Final scene should end with a concrete verdict, warning, or useful next thought, not an empty slogan.
visualPlan: For every scene, state why selectedPattern fits the narration.
Add a visualTwist: a composition, metaphor, motion, or source-media treatment specific to this story.
Avoid repeating the same card stack on adjacent scenes.
Prefer brand marks, product logos, source screenshots, metrics, diagrams, and icons over restating narration.
html: Final visible state must remain on screen until scene end.
No important text or focal visual may enter the bottom subtitle gap.
Canvas text must be short labels in the selected video language, not paragraphs.
If using local media, make it large enough to inspect and do not cover it with explanatory text.

themeMoods.notes: Premium market board: dark base, amber highlights, cyan data accents, green/red state semantics.
Quiet terminal/system mood; good for terminal-card, cpu-chip, data-stream.
Escbase starter-like contrast: warm hero accent plus cool technical secondary.
Use sparingly for final-lockup, proof-frame, or high-stakes verdict.

backgroundFx.notes: Small low-opacity particles, never distract from main visual.
Soft scan lines or panels; keep behind content.
Directional light streams or curved paths that make process movement visible.
Radial rings around a central mark or icon.
Elegant attractor-like motion; keep thin and decorative behind the main composition.
Subtle grain/noise, not dirty or low-quality.
Use clean background when media must be inspected.

useWhen: one base visual plus 2-3 highlighted modules
revealModes.mapping: Base visual first, then each highlighted card/module.
Base visual first, then red, yellow, green states.

useWhen: many items connected to one core
motion: chips bob or reveal around orbit
patterns.htmlHints: Use a large central visual in the middle third. Keep chips short and readable. Scene 1 should usually use one hero element plus hook, not a full explainer.
patterns.exampleUse: Binance at center, US stock tickers and stablecoin chips orbit around it.

patterns.htmlHints: Each lane should contain a keyword, icon/logo, and one short supporting label. Do not put paragraphs inside lanes.
patterns.exampleUse: Wall Street/traditional finance lane versus Web3/blockchain finance lane.

patterns.htmlHints: Use 3-4 steps max. Each step should have a number/icon and short label. If text becomes long, split scene or move detail to narration.
patterns.exampleUse: Real stock asset -> bStocks token -> DeFi borrowing/liquidity.

patterns.htmlHints: Make the number dominant. Supporting text must be much smaller. One scene should not contain more than 3 metrics.
patterns.exampleUse: $5 minimum order and 0% trading fee.

patterns.htmlHints: Use 2x2 or vertical 3-card grid for 9:16. Each tile is number + label only.
patterns.exampleUse: 35% cheaper, 59% fewer tokens, 49% faster, 70% fewer calls.

patterns.htmlHints: Use 2-4 rows max. Labels must be short. Do not create a dense table; the bar lengths should tell the story.
patterns.exampleUse: ETF inflow vs outflow, stock index vs crypto index, fee before vs after.

patterns.htmlHints: Good as a focused identity block. Keep the provider/status secondary and avoid long descriptions.
patterns.exampleUse: One Binance product badge with a small 'Tokenized stocks' status tag.

patterns.htmlHints: Each state must have a short label and icon. Use red/yellow/green semantics clearly. Do not make warning text tiny.
patterns.exampleUse: 2021 stopped by legal pressure -> uncertainty -> 2024 upgraded comeback.

patterns.htmlHints: Card text should stay under two lines. Keep risk cards high enough above subtitle zone.
patterns.exampleUse: Legal risk, market risk, liquidity risk.

patterns.htmlHints: Make source media large and clean. Do not cover it with explanatory text. Attribution should be short.
patterns.exampleUse: A Binance announcement screenshot shown as source proof.

patterns.htmlHints: Use local media only. Keep demo large. Do not add backstage labels like downloaded/muted/local file.
patterns.exampleUse: A captured demo of an app or exchange UI.

patterns.htmlHints: Use monospace for terminal only. Keep commands short and legible.
patterns.exampleUse: npm install, API request, render command.

patterns.htmlHints: The gauge is the visual. Put only one short number/label beside it.
patterns.exampleUse: Render speed or transaction speed improvement.

patterns.htmlHints: Good for invisible backend flow. Use labels sparingly.
patterns.exampleUse: Stablecoin value moving into tokenized stock market access.

patterns.htmlHints: Use as a metaphor for backend/system mechanics. One short caption only; let the visual carry the technical feel.
patterns.exampleUse: An engine-like chip showing tokenization or settlement infrastructure.

patterns.htmlHints: Use 3-5 connectors max. Each connector is icon plus 1-3 words. Good alternative to repetitive cards.
patterns.exampleUse: Stablecoin, stocks, DeFi, and exchange access connecting into one Binance lane.

patterns.htmlHints: Each bubble is one short idea. Avoid fake quotes unless source provides them.
patterns.exampleUse: Mixed market reactions to Binance bStocks.

useWhen: three risk states need a strong visual metaphor
patterns.htmlHints: Use when traffic-light should look like a real object, not just cards. Labels must be short and aligned with the lamps.
patterns.exampleUse: Regulatory stop -> uncertainty -> controlled relaunch.

patterns.htmlHints: Use 3-4 events max. Keep dates/labels short; do not turn it into a text-heavy history slide.
patterns.exampleUse: 2021 pause, 2024 comeback, 2026 product expansion.

useWhen: needs subtle motion behind a central visual
patterns.htmlHints: This is a supporting pattern, not the whole slide. Keep opacity low so text/media stays readable.
patterns.exampleUse: A market-system background behind a Binance or stock-token hero mark.

patterns.htmlHints: Use when the visual comparison matters. Keep each card mostly graphic; feedback text should be one short phrase.
patterns.exampleUse: Traditional broker screen vs tokenized-stock exchange screen.

patterns.htmlHints: The final line should be memorable and short. Do not end with a generic slogan if risk/context matters.
patterns.exampleUse: Balanced warning plus follow/update CTA.
